cards.back: A graph traversal algorithm that explores a graph or tree by visiting a node and then visiting all of its neighbors before backtracking

cards.front: How does Depth First Search work?
cards.back: It starts at a given node and explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Depth First Search?
cards.back: O(|E| + |V|) in the worst case, where |E| is the number of edges and |V| is the number of vertices

cards.front: Is Depth First Search suitable for unweighted graphs?
cards.back: Yes, it is particularly efficient for unweighted graphs

cards.front: What is the main advantage of Depth First Search?
cards.back: It can be easily used to detect cycles in a graph and find the strongly connected components
